samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">Set to 8 to represent an 8-bit multiplier.</parameter>
        <parameter id="N_1">Set to 7 to represent the number of bits minus one for an 8-bit multiplier.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_en_out_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_en_in for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_en_in"/>
            <output id="mul_en_out_reg"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>On the positive edge of the clock.</description>
                    <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                </condition>
                <true>
                    <description>Update mul_en_out_reg with the value of mul_en_in.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>mul_en_out_reg</id>
                            <value>
                                <binary_operator id="mul_en_in" op="ASSIGN">
                                    <id>mul_en_in</id>
                                </binary_operator>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </true>
            </always>
            <output id="mul_en_out">
                <description>Output signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled.</description>
                <expression>
                    <binary_operator id="mul_en_out_reg" op="AND">
                        <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                        <id>mul_en_out_reg</id>
                    </binary_operator>
                </expression>
            </output>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_a_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_a for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_a"/>
            <output id="mul_a_reg"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>On the positive edge of the clock.</description>
                    <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                </condition>
                <true>
                    <description>Update mul_a_reg with the value of mul_a.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>mul_a_reg</id>
                            <value>
                                <binary_operator id="mul_a" op="ASSIGN">
                                    <id>mul_a</id>
                                </binary_operator>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </true>
            </always>
            <output id="mul_a_reg">
                <description>Output signal representing the multiplicand.</description>
                <expression>
                    <binary_operator id="mul_a_reg" op="AND">
                        <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                        <id>mul_a_reg</id>
                    </binary_operator>
                </expression>
            </output>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="mul_b_reg" type="sequential_logic">
            <description>Register to store the value of mul_b for use in the next clock cycle.</description>
            <input id="mul_b"/>
            <output id="mul_b_reg"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>On the positive edge of the clock.</description>
                    <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                </condition>
                <true>
                    <description>Update mul_b_reg with the value of mul_b.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>mul_b_reg</id>
                            <value>
                                <binary_operator id="mul_b" op="ASSIGN">
                                    <id>mul_b</id>
                                </binary_operator>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </true>
            </always>
            <output id="mul_b_reg">
                <description>Output signal representing the multiplier.</description>
                <expression>
                    <binary_operator id="mul_b_reg" op="AND">
                        <constant id="clk" value="1"/>
                        <id>mul_b_reg</id>
                    </binary_operator>
                </expression>
            </output>
        </sequential_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[0]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate the first partial product.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg"/>
            <output id="temp[0]"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>When the first bit of the multiplier is 1.</description>
                    <binary_operator id="mul_b_reg[0]" op="EQUAL">
                        <constant id="mul_b_reg[0]" value="1"/>
                    </binary_operator>
                </condition>
                <true>
                    <description>Select the multiplicand as the partial product.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>temp[0]</id>
                            <value>
                                <binary_operator id="mul_a_reg" op="ASSIGN">
                                    <id>mul_a_reg</id>
                                </binary_operator>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <description>Select 0 as the partial product.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>temp[0]</id>
                            <value>
                                <constant id="temp[0]" value="0"/>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </false>
            </always>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[1]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate the second partial product.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg"/>
            <output id="temp[1]"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>When the second bit of the multiplier is 1.</description>
                    <binary_operator id="mul_b_reg[1]" op="EQUAL">
                        <constant id="mul_b_reg[1]" value="1"/>
                    </binary_operator>
                </condition>
                <true>
                    <description>Select the multiplicand shifted left by 1 as the partial product.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>temp[1]</id>
                            <value>
                                <binary_operator id="mul_a_reg" op="ASSIGN">
                                    <id>mul_a_reg</id>
                                </binary_operator>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <description>Select 0 as the partial product.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>temp[1]</id>
                            <value>
                                <constant id="temp[1]" value="0"/>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </false>
            </always>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[2]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate the third partial product.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg"/>
            <output id="temp[2]"/>
            <always>
                <condition>
                    <description>When the third bit of the multiplier is 1.</description>
                    <binary_operator id="mul_b_reg[2]" op="EQUAL">
                        <constant id="mul_b_reg[2]" value="1"/>
                    </binary_operator>
                </condition>
                <true>
                    <description>Select the multiplicand shifted left by 2 as the partial product.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>temp[2]</id>
                            <value>
                                <binary_operator id="mul_a_reg" op="ASSIGN">
                                    <id>mul_a_reg</id>
                                </binary_operator>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </true>
                <false>
                    <description>Select 0 as the partial product.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <nonblocking>
                            <id>temp[2]</id>
                            <value>
                                <constant id="temp[2]" value="0"/>
                            </value>
                        </nonblocking>
                    </statement>
                </false>
            </always>
        </combinational_logic>
        <combinational_logic id="temp[3]" type="combinational_logic">
            <description>Combinational logic to generate the fourth partial product.</description>
            <input id="mul_a_reg"/>
            <input id="mul_b_reg
